_rationale: code_insight mode=git_hotspots. INTENTIONAL DIVERGENCE: like git_analyze, Go computes the change-frequency report then DISCARDS it (formatStatisticsSection ignores the GitHotspots field) and prints an all-zero STATISTICS block. C++ surfaces real churn data in a == GIT HOTSPOTS == section (top changed files, collision zones), sourced from git::FrequencyAnalyzer — the ONLY surface for that engine, which is otherwise dead code in both ports. Output reflects a rolling time window over LIVE git history, so it is inherently non-deterministic (commits age out of the window over time); content[].text cannot be byte-locked. Real-content correctness is locked C++-side by CodeInsightGitTest against a controlled git fixture with a wide window.

tiers._rationale_stable: JSON-RPC envelope + content type only. result.content[].text is intentional C++ enrichment Go discards and is time-window-volatile, so it is held in the ignore tier.
tiers._rationale_ignore: result.content[].text: C++ emits real change-frequency hotspots (== GIT HOTSPOTS ==) where Go's formatter drops the data and prints zero STATISTICS — no common subset, and the values shift as the rolling time window advances over live history. Universal MCP descriptor noise (id/jsonrpc/meta) handled by the centralized baseline.
